Property tours are available by appointment only on Thursday, April 16, 2026 from 12 pm – 2 pm. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.
   
Grand Union Plaza offers investors an exceptional opportunity to acquire an 85,453-square-foot shopping center situated in Norwich, New York.
 
Located in the heart of downtown, 54 E Main Street is a well-kept neighborhood retail center anchored by a dark Grand Union Supermarket, whose lease runs through March 31, 2030—providing four years of guaranteed income while an investor secures a new replacement anchor. The center features a diverse mix of shops, service providers, and medical offices, creating steady daily traffic and a convenient destination for the surrounding community. Originally built in 1997 and renovated in 2004, the property maintains strong curb appeal and offers ample on-site parking. With approximately 9,000 square feet of leasable space still available, the asset presents a compelling value-add opportunity: bringing the vacancy to market rents can meaningfully enhance returns while strengthening the center’s long-term tenant mix.
 
Grand Union Plaza features excellent frontage on E Main Street, with convenient customer access from Front Street and Birdsall Street. As a key retail center in the area, this high-profile property presents an attractive opportunity for investors. It is located within walking distance of Perkins School of the Arts, the Chenango County Fairgrounds, and Norwich High School. Other notable nearby destinations include SUNY Morrisville | Norwich Campus, UHS Chenango Memorial Hospital, and Weller Park. Just one block from S Broad Street—the town's main retail corridor, seeing 15,053 vehicles daily—customers have easy access to a variety of specialty shops and local restaurants. Local businesses in the vicinity include Norwich Deli and Shop, Deja Brew, and Charlie's Diner. Grand Union Plaza is positioned in the center of it all.
 
This site in Norwich, New York, presents investors with the opportunity to make a meaningful contribution to a thriving small-town community. The area benefits from a stable resident population, with a majority of owner-occupied homes reflecting a strong local commitment. Norwich serves as a central hub for Chenango County, providing a primary destination for residents to fulfill their shopping needs.

brokerDetails.tagLine: Retail & Shopping Center Specialist
brokerDetails.title: Licensed Associate Real Estate Broker
brokerDetails.bio:
Joseph French boasts an extensive career spanning over 30 years, marked by proficiency in development, asset and property management, leasing, and investment sales. With a track record encompassing sales of more than 300 properties totaling over 25 million square feet and exceeding $5 billion in value, French has navigated various market cycles with finesse.
 
A respected figure in the industry, Mr. French’s insights have been featured in prominent publications such as Shopping Centers Today, GlobeSt.com, Retail Traffic, CCIM Magazine, and National Real Estate Investor. He has also graced numerous industry conferences as a featured speaker. Mr. French has received numerous awards over the years. A few of his most recent include the “Best of White Plains Award” for the 9th consecutive year in the Real Estate & Construction category, as well as being selected as one of the New York & Tri-State winners in Connect CRE’s 2024 Top Broker Awards.
 
French also consistently ranked among Sperry Van Ness’ Top 10 Brokers and received multiple National Achievement Awards from Marcus & Millichap highlighting his exceptional contributions. Holding the esteemed CCIM designation and real estate broker licenses in New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Connecticut, and Massachusetts, French brings a wealth of expertise to his role. Before joining Marcus & Millichap, he served in key leadership positions at Sperry Van Ness and The Hutensky Group and also founded his own successful firm focusing on urban retail projects. His previous roles at renowned firms like Landau & Heyman, Brandenberg Realty, and CS Brown Associates further enrich his extensive professional background.
 
In March, 2020, French authored a book titled “French Lessons: Letters to my Daughters”, which combines an autobiographical portrait of a highly successful, real-estate investment adviser with advice about business and life, communicated through letters from French to his three adult daughters.

description:
Ripco Real Estate has been retained on an exclusive basis to arrange the sale of 5300 East 23rd Street #1C, New York, New York, 10010 — a retail condominium featuring an investment-grade Bank of America branch and a vacant lower level offering future income upside.
   
The ground floor is occupied by Bank of America, operating under a long-term lease and serving as a full-service retail banking branch. The lower level is currently vacant, presenting investors with the opportunity to generate supplemental income through future lease-up.
   
Located in the heart of Gramercy Park, one of Manhattan’s most historic and affluent neighborhoods, the property is positioned along East 23rd Street — a major crosstown retail corridor benefiting from strong pedestrian and vehicular traffic. The surrounding area features a dense mix of residential, office, and institutional demand drivers.
   
The property offers excellent access to public transportation, including the New York City Subway 6 train at 23rd Street, the N and R trains at 23rd Street (Broadway Line), and the L train at 3rd Avenue, along with multiple bus lines, including the M23 SBS crosstown route, M9, and M34, providing seamless connectivity throughout Manhattan.
   
Nearby national retailers and neighborhood anchors include Target, Trader Joe's, CVS, Starbucks, and Chase Bank, complemented by a strong mix of restaurants, fitness operators, and service retail. The property also benefits from proximity to notable landmarks, including Gramercy Park, Madison Square Park, and the Flatiron Building, driving consistent foot traffic and neighborhood activity.
   
For more information, please contact the exclusive brokers directly.

Investors seeking a top-tier opportunity in Downtown Akron, Ohio, will be drawn to the potential of 315 E Market Street.
  
Boasting 10,003 square feet on a single level, this retail strip center stands out as a prime opportunity in Akron’s dynamic market. Originally constructed in 1956, the property enjoys exceptional visibility along E Market Street and benefits from convenient access via a signalized intersection. Ample on-site parking serves both customers and employees, further enhancing the location’s appeal and ease of use. Featuring an established tenant and adaptable floor plans, the center is well-positioned to attract a diverse range of businesses. Configured for three tenants, the suites offer flexible sizing to accommodate various needs. One suite is currently leased to PPG Paints through 2030, providing a reliable income stream. Investors have the option to upgrade the property and lease the remaining suites at market rates, or utilize part of the space for their own business while collecting rent from other tenants. This asset presents excellent potential for both income growth and operational flexibility.
 
This property is strategically located within Akron, offering exceptional proximity to key amenities. Summa Health System – Akron, a 350-bed hospital, is situated just down E Market Street. Additionally, the University of Akron campus lies only 1 mile away, providing convenient access for tenants and contributing to the vibrancy of the local economy through its student population. Numerous cultural and entertainment venues, including the Akron Art Museum, John S Knight Center, Akron Civic Theater, and Akron Children’s Museum, are also nearby. The area benefits from a dynamic mix of national and local retailers and restaurants that enhance the quality of life for residents and visitors alike.
 
Akron stands out as an attractive destination for investors. The city has evolved from tire production to become a leader in polymer science, advanced manufacturing, and healthcare. The Akron metropolitan area generates roughly $44.6 billion in GDP each year, placing it among America’s mid-sized metro economies. With Fortune 500 companies like Goodyear and FirstEnergy headquartered in Akron, the city maintains a strong corporate presence. All in all, Akron offers a diverse economy built on innovation, healthcare, and manufacturing, backed by notable research institutions and affordable living costs.

brokerDetails.name: Michael Glass
brokerDetails.title: Executive Managing Director/Chief Revenue Officer
brokerDetails.bio:
Michael L. Glass is senior vice president and division manager for Marcus & Millichap’s Midwest Division, which includes 12 brokerage offices. He also serves as national director of the Manufactured Housing Division of Marcus & Millichap.

Mr. Glass joined the firm in 2001 under its sales intern program and became an investment associate specializing in multi-family.  He joined the management team in June 2006 as Sales Manager of the Chicago office. He was promoted to Regional Manager of the Cleveland office in 2007 and took on additional responsibility for the Columbus office in 2009 and the Cincinnati office in 2013. During this period of time, Glass led the Cleveland to become one of the top offices throughout the firm. He was appointed district manager in 2016 expanding his oversight to five offices. He became senior vice president and division manager in 2019. 

During his time with the firm, Mr. Glass has been involved with and overseen thousands of transactions totaling several billion dollars along with leading the first acquisition post IPO for the firm.

Prior to working with Marcus & Millichap, Mr. Glass was a credit analyst with LaSalle Bank/ABM AMRO.

description:
Keller Williams Realty is pleased to exclusively present the incredible opportunity to acquire the fee simple interest in the Baymont by Wyndham Jacksonville Airport, located at 1153 Airport Road. Offering 214 keys with a prime location near Jacksonville International Airport and Interstate 95, this rare opportunity allows investors to claim a prime position in Jacksonville’s thriving tourism industry. 
 
Baymont by Wyndham Jacksonville Airport, originally built in 1973 and fully renovated in 2024, spans a total of 97,130 square feet. The two-story, exterior corridor property sets itself apart from competitors with a robust amenity package, including an outdoor pool, a business center, a meeting event space, a convenience mart, shuttle service to the airport, and a free hot breakfast buffet. Additionally, the property is just minutes from the sprawling River City Marketplace shopping center, presenting a wealth of convenient off-site amenities. It welcomes both individual travelers and groups, with abundant on-site parking for vehicles, buses, and RVs. 
 
Placed only 2 miles from Jacksonville International Airport and just off Interstate 95, the hotel serves as the perfect option for visitors traveling to Jacksonville’s most popular destinations, including Jacksonville Zoo and Gardens, EverBank Stadium, Jacksonville Beach, and the JAXPORT cruise terminal. More than 8 million annual visitors flock to the city to visit these hotspots, driving an economic impact of more than $7.4 billion. Additionally, the property is just 12 miles from the Prime F Osborn III Convention Center, delivering an added audience of business travelers. 
 
Combining an income-producing hotel asset with a premier location near major transportation hubs in a popular tourist market, Baymont by Wyndham Jacksonville Airport presents the perfect acquisition for savvy investors. 
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

Dino Monteverde is a hotel broker specializing exclusively in hospitality investment sales across the United States. As part of Sarhan Hotel Group, he advises hotel owners, investors, and operators on acquisitions, dispositions, and strategic positioning of limited-service, select-service, full-service, and resort assets.

Dino entered hotel brokerage without a traditional finance background or institutional training and has built his practice through disciplined owner outreach, market research, and long-term relationship management. Over the past several years, he has been involved in the execution of multiple hotel transactions and currently manages a large pipeline of on- and off-market hospitality opportunities.

Prior to brokerage, Dino was a hotel owner and operator, having founded and scaled a multi-location hostel business before exiting in 2020. His operational background provides a practical perspective on valuation, deal structure, and execution beyond theory.

Dino is a U.S. Marine Corps veteran and brings a process-driven, execution-focused approach to hospitality real estate, emphasizing discretion, consistency, and alignment between buyers and sellers.

description:
Landmark Apartments, located at 3299 S Polk Street in Dallas, Texas, offers investors the opportunity to acquire a 60-unit market-rate multifamily asset in one of the nation’s top real estate investment markets at a low basis, with meaningful operational upside. The property is currently 88% occupied and offers some of the largest one- and two-bedroom floor plans in the area for its class. The unit mix comprises 33 one-bedroom and 27 two-bedroom units, averaging $1,190 in rent across occupied units, with an average unit size of approximately 757 square feet. Situated on 2.35 acres in an infill location, the asset consists of five buildings and provides ample parking, including 94 total spaces, 36 of which are covered, generating an additional $468 per month in parking income.
  
The property presents a clear value-add opportunity through improved operational efficiencies. It is currently operating at an approximate 75% expense ratio, which is above market for comparable assets in this class and submarket, with identifiable opportunities to reduce expenses. One strategy worth exploring is transitioning to a centralized management structure, budgeting payroll for one full-time maintenance technician only, eliminating an on-site manager, and instead allocating for a higher third-party management fee and leasing commissions. This approach could streamline operations while maintaining leasing performance. We have also obtained bids from a reputable general contractor outlining capital improvements that address immediate deferred maintenance, enhance curb appeal, and reduce repair and maintenance expenses over time. With successful execution of the business plan, we are projecting the potential to return more than 50% of initial equity through a refinance within approximately 24 months.
 
Situated in Oak Cliff, just under 4 miles south of the Bishop Arts District, Landmark Apartments offers a conveniently accessible property for tenants. Positioned with easy connectivity to major arteries like Interstate 35, US-67, and Loop 12, the property is an ideal choice for those commuting across the metroplex. Its location places it a mere 10-minute drive from both Downtown Dallas and the University of North Texas at Dallas. Residents also enjoy the convenience of gas stations, grocery stores, restaurants, pharmacies, and recreation at Kiest Park, all within the immediate vicinity.
 
Supporting steady growth in the submarket, Oak Cliff provides a compelling demographic profile of potential residents when looking within just 3 miles of Landmark Apartments. The area is home to a population of more than 133,000 with an average household income of over $59,000, along with more than 25,000 daytime employees. Residents of the area contribute more than $1.09 billion in consumer spending, including more than $163 million spent on household costs. This valuable base ensures a climate of sustained growth for quality multifamily inventory like Landmark Apartments. 
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
1950 University Avenue presents an exceptional opportunity for investors, developers, or owner/users seeking a turnkey property within Berkeley's dynamic downtown district. Situated on 0.31 acres, this 20,747-square-foot office building features two stories with open floor plans designed to accommodate diverse tenant requirements. The property features an upgraded common area, atrium skylights that bring in natural light, and on-site parking. A new HVAC system ensures comfort throughout, while private decks and a spacious outdoor terrace on the second floor provide flexible indoor and outdoor workspace options for employees. Inside, the building includes a training room, multiple private offices, a conference room, cubicles, a kitchen, dedicated file storage, and five parking spaces, as well as a deck area. The interior currently consists of generous open areas that can be readily reconfigured to align with a new owner's vision. Zoned C-DMU (Downtown Mixed-Use), the site aligns with the city's Downtown Area Plan adopted in 2012, supporting objectives such as environmental sustainability, land use, accessibility, historic preservation, urban design, housing and community health, and economic development. This designation offers buyers significant flexibility for future use.
 
Situated in the heart of Downtown Berkeley, this property enjoys a prime location on University Avenue. It offers convenient access to major thoroughfares as well as proximity to public transit, including the Downtown Berkeley Station. The surrounding area is a dynamic center for arts, dining, retail, business, and entertainment. The presence of the University of California, Berkeley, just a few blocks away, significantly contributes to the local environment. Prominent cultural venues such as the Roda Theatre, Berkeley Repertory Theatre, Aurora Theatre Company, Berkeley Art Museum and Pacific Film Archive, and the UC Theatre Taube Family Music Hall are all nearby. In addition, several well-regarded restaurants, including Ippudo Berkeley, Imm Thai Street Food, The Butcher’s Son, and Platano, are located in close vicinity.
 
The East Bay represents a strong market for commercial real estate investment. Over the past year, median incomes have risen, reaching $130,000, outpacing the national average growth rate. Prominent employers in the area include Workday, Lam Research, and Veeva Systems, with recent additions such as fintech companies Square and Credit Karma further diversifying the business landscape. The ongoing expansion of the BART transit system connecting the East Bay to San Jose is enhancing commuting options for residents, increasing accessibility to major job centers.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
Berkeley Capital Advisors is proud to offer the Piggly Wiggly located at 208 E McIntyre Street in Mullins, South Carolina. 
 
Built in 2005, this property has long served the local community. The single-story building covers roughly 60,000 square feet and features an open, flexible floor plan. Set on 3.5 acres, the site provides 133 surface parking spaces for both staff and customers. This Piggly Wiggly is one of 14 stores operated by Lowcountry Grocers, a seasoned local grocer committed to success with its hands-on approach and “Local Since Forever” philosophy. The South Park location reported $8.2 million in sales through 2025, offering buyers an attractive income-producing investment.
 
208 E McIntyre Street is ideally situated for investors looking for their next opportunity. Located in Mullins, a small historic city once known as the tobacco capital, the area is celebrated for its peaceful, rural charm, vibrant downtown, and attractions like the SC Tobacco Museum. The property sits along a busy retail corridor, making the Piggly Wiggly easily visible to passing consumers. It’s conveniently located just 10 miles from Route 501, providing straightforward regional access. Situated in Marion County, the site is close to several points of interest. It’s only 15 minutes from Inland Port Dillon, a $58 million, 3,400-acre intermodal facility operated by SC Ports that connects local importers and exporters to the Port of Charleston via CSX rail, and just 45 minutes from Myrtle Beach.
 
Mullins offers strong investment fundamentals, with a stable population, a high rate of owner-occupied homes, and a solid local workforce of more than 7,800 employees within 10 miles. These factors provide consistent consumer demand and long-term economic stability, making the area an appealing choice for investors.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
As the exclusive advisor, Nexus Realty Advisors has been retained by ownership to offer the hands-off flex warehouse investment opportunity at 160 Belmont Avenue in Garfield, New Jersey, to the market. 
    
A 23,750-square-foot flex warehouse improves the 0.43-acre parcel fronting rail access, currently 100% occupied by regional tenants. Each tenant has in-place escalations through their lease term, ranging from 2027 to 2029. Net operating income exceeds $260,000 annually, making this a stabilized asset with future upside and additional leasing momentum to tap into. 
    
The building includes a front-loading station, storage space, drive-ins, dock-high doors, a mezzanine level, and over 25 surface parking spaces. The second story is window-lined to bring plenty of natural light into the office space, while the warehouse boasts high ceilings.

description:
221 Washington Street and 60 Henry Street are strategically positioned in the heart of Downtown Binghamton. This asset represents a high-density cluster of student housing and mixed-use buildings located within one of Binghamton’s most active rental markets. The property benefits from proximity to Binghamton University’s downtown campus, Government Plaza, Mirabito Stadium, and the city’s growing retail and restaurant scene. The site is within walking distance of the SUNY bus line and public transportation, ensuring strong accessibility and sustained tenant demand. Zoned R3 or Commercial, the property provides operational flexibility for continued student housing use or mixed-use repositioning, with consistent occupancy, proven rent growth, and a central downtown footprint.
    
Situated in the heart of Downtown Binghamton, 221 Washington Street and 60 Henry Street benefit from direct access to the city’s strongest demand drivers. The properties are surrounded by a vibrant mix of restaurants, entertainment venues, retail, and services that cater to both students and professionals. The location places residents within walking distance of Binghamton University’s downtown campus, SUNY Broome bus lines, Government Plaza, and major employers, including UHS and Lourdes Hospital. 
    
Downtown Binghamton continues to undergo steady revitalization, driven by university expansion, private investment, and the ongoing redevelopment of historic buildings into modern housing and commercial spaces. With proximity to major roadways, including Route 434, Interstate 81, and Interstate 86, the area offers seamless regional connectivity while maintaining a true urban lifestyle. The Downtown Core remains one of the most walkable and in-demand neighborhoods in the city, ensuring long-term appeal for both residents and investors.
    
221 Washington Street and 60 Henry Street offer investors a strong in-place income stream with long-term appreciation potential supported by Binghamton’s expanding university and medical sectors.

brokerDetails.tagLine: Scott Warren specializes in office and industrial properties.
brokerDetails.title: CCIM, CRE Team Lic. RE Salesperson, Member NYSCAR
brokerDetails.bio: Scott has had a great career in sales and marketing. Scott began a financial services career in 1986. After several years as an agent, field manager, and district manager, he began as a regional sales director for Columbian Mutual Life Insurance Company. He started with the territory of upstate NY and eventually was in charge of the entire Northeast. He then, at a young age, moved into a Corporate Executive position and prior to leaving the company in 2005 was the Second Vice President of Agency Development, handling all home office management for three sales divisions and approximately 16,000 agents. Much of the growth of Columbian Life Insurance Company can be attributed to Scott’s sales and marketing efforts. Scott then began an independent marketing organization, Niche Market Insurers. With the help and much credit going to his two partners, Scott built an independent marketing organization from scratch to one with a nationwide footprint of 7,000 agents and over $5 million dollars a year in new annual premium. This gave Scott a very solid foundation for Investment analysis as well as in sales and marketing. Scott sold his share of the marketing organization to fully involve himself in the commercial real estate field. Starting his career with Pyramid Brokerage Company/Cushman and Wakefield in a very short time Scott made an immediate impact in the Binghamton Market. Scott has been involved in the sale of commercial buildings, a number of investment-based sales, redevelopment projects, and most notably the sale of the Grand Royal Hotel in Downtown Binghamton. Scott currently represents some of the area’s largest investors and developers. Scott has earned certificates through the CCIM Institute in foundations in commercial real estate, financial analysis, reliable valuations, 1031 exchange, and negotiations. Scott is also currently enrolled in and working towards a certificate in commercial real estate from the prestigious Cornell University’s SC Johnson School of business.

brokerDetails.bio:
James Lucenti is a licensed real estate salesperson with SVN® Innovative Commercial Advisors, specializing in commercial real estate. With a Masters in School Psychology from Buffalo University and an additional Masters in School Administration, James brings a wealth of knowledge and a strong educational background to his real estate career. With an impressive 24 years of experience in education, he has demonstrated dedication and expertise in helping others achieve their goals.
Prior to his career in commercial real estate, James spent 6 years in residential real estate, gaining valuable experience and insight into the intricacies of the real estate market. Originally from Fairport, NY, he has called the Binghamton area home since 2004. James is a devoted father with two children, and he also has a personal investment in the real estate market, owning a multi-family property in Endwell.


With over six years of experience in residential real estate, James has developed a keen understanding of the industry's dynamics, market trends, and the nuances that can significantly impact property transactions. His hands-on experience, coupled with his educational background, equips him with a unique skill set that benefits his clients in commercial real estate endeavors.

Effective communication is a cornerstone of James's approach, and he actively listens to your unique needs and goals. When you work with James, you benefit from his extensive educational background, years of experience in real estate, and his deep connection to the Binghamton community. His commitment to your success and loyalty to your interests set him apart as a trusted partner in the commercial real estate industry.

description:
Milestone Real Estate Company is pleased to present the user-ready warehouse opportunity at 44052 Yost Road in Canton, Michigan, to the market. The building spans 55,000 square feet and offers three 10-foot by 12-foot drive-ins, two exterior loading docks, and 3-phase power. The 9.7-acre lot has abundant yardage for storage, parking, and large-vehicle maneuvering.
     
44052 Yost Road is ideally located within an industrial neighborhood approximately 30 miles west of Detroit. The subject property is surrounded by industrial neighbors such as regional contractors, distributors, and manufacturers. Nearby are a FedEx sorting facility, an O’Reilly Auto Parts distribution center, and the Great Lakes Coca-Cola Co. bottling center. The interchange of Interstates 275 and 94 is 6.5 miles south, outside of the Detroit Metro Wayne County Airport, Detroit’s primary international airport.
     
Wayne County is a predominantly blue-collar region, with approximately 64,817 daytime employees reporting to warehouse operations within 10-mile radius. 44052 Yost Road is in the county’s industrial corridor, where the workforce is highly skilled.
     
The Detroit metro has a rich industrial heritage and an established ecosystem for tech and entrepreneurship. State investment in key areas like education, business development, and innovation will allow industries like semiconductors, artificial intelligence, electric vehicles, and renewable energy to thrive. Currently, Michigan is home to one-third of the nation's battery production. By the end of the decade, it's expected to be one of the top three electric vehicle battery manufacturing states.
     
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the broker to schedule.

brokerDetails.name: Ali Haidar
brokerDetails.tagLine: In December 2024, Haidar launched and now leads the Commercial Real Estate Division under Milestone Real Estate Company.
brokerDetails.title: President & Director of Operations
brokerDetails.bio:
Ali N. Haidar began his career at the age of nineteen in residential real estate. In 2006, he devoted months to learning the business and quickly became a Multi-Million Dollar Top Producer. In 2008, Haidar expanded his business into the REO arena, gaining clients such as Wells Fargo, Ocwen, and Titanium. In 2011, he acquired his Real Estate Broker’s license and established a residential real estate firm, where he supervised associates in all phases of brokerage operations while handling acquisitions and dispositions of distressed properties on behalf of investors. Later that year, he expanded into property management, forming a company that managed assets for clients including Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

In 2014, Haidar joined NAI Farbman as an Associate Broker, transitioning into the commercial real estate sector with a focus on acquisitions, dispositions, land development, and leasing. Over time, he developed specialized expertise in medical, clinical healthcare, dental, mental health, autism centers, government facilities, and corporate negotiations on behalf of property owners.

In December 2024, Haidar stepped down from his role at Farbman to launch and lead the Commercial Real Estate Division under Milestone Real Estate Company, a firm he originally founded in 2012. Under his leadership as President & Director of Operations, Milestone is now a full-service commercial real estate firm offering tailored strategies, seamless transactions, and innovative solutions across multiple asset classes.

description:
JLL is pleased to offer for sale a fee simple interest in the freestanding restaurant property at 2624 Music Valley Drive in Nashville, Tennessee. The property consists of a 17,017-square-foot restaurant with a complete dining infrastructure on a 2.97-acre parcel. This acquisition opportunity is in the Nashville MSA, one of the most desirable real estate markets in the country. 
     
2624 Music Valley Drive is located 7 miles northeast of Downtown Nashville, strategically positioned with excellent exposure and visibility to Briley Parkway, which sees over 84,000 vehicles per day. The property sits less than a mile from Opry Mills, Tennessee’s most extensive retail shopping, dining, and entertainment destination, with over 200 retailers. Additionally, the area is home to the Gaylord Opryland Resort and Convention Center, the nation’s largest non-gaming hotel/convention center under one roof. Together, these two destinations welcome over 4 million visitors annually and are among Nashville's premier attractions among its robust and growing tourist population. 
     
As a regional hub, Nashville is served by Interstates 24, 65, and 40, which provide access to neighboring communities. Urban areas are accessible through the 440 Parkway, Downtown Loop, and Briley Parkway, while Route 840 connects four counties in an outer loop around the city. Public transportation options include WeGo Public Transit buses and the Music City Star Commuter Rail. Travelers going farther out of town enjoy convenient access to the highly rated Nashville International Airport (7 miles), which offers hundreds of regional and international flights daily.
     
2624 Music Valley Drive has a strong, growing demographic pool within a 5-mile radius. More than 141,600 residents live in households with an average income of $82,400 or more. Each year, the realm generates more than $1.7 billion in consumer spending, contributing to the local economy. Food and drinks account for $454 million of the spending power, while entertainment totals nearly $3 million. 
     
Nashville is one of the fastest-growing metropolitan statistical areas in the United States, with approximately 2 million residents. The state of Tennessee is business-friendly, with no state income tax, benefiting business owners alike. Inquire today and learn more information about acquiring a one-of-a-kind retail asset in Tennessee’s Music City.

description:
6401-6403 Erdman Avenue presents buyers with a hands-off investment opportunity in Baltimore, Maryland.
 
Covering 28,421 square feet on a single floor, this well-maintained brick property offers easy upkeep and appeals to buyers seeking hassle-free ownership in industrial outdoor storage (IOS) buildings. The industrial facility boasts two drive-in bays, 20-foot ceilings, and plentiful parking, all situated on a large 1.23-acre lot that includes a secure fenced yard and turnaround space for big vehicles. The site is zoned I-2. The building currently generates steady cash flow with minimal management required, as it is fully leased to A-Tech Hydraulics and O-Go Sales, both reliable tenants with solid occupancy records. Both leases are NNN, allowing owners to enjoy a truly passive investment. 
 
This property is strategically located within Baltimore, enabling highly efficient operations. Direct access to Interstates 95 and 895 facilitates rapid connectivity both throughout the city and to major metropolitan areas, including Washington, DC, Philadelphia, New York, and others. The site’s position along a rail line further enhances logistical efficiency. Martin State Airport is conveniently accessible by car. The proximity to major thoroughfares ensures convenient commuting for employees, while an abundance of retail and dining establishments nearby provides excellent options for pre-work, post-work, and lunch breaks.
 
Baltimore is considered an attractive market for investors. The region continues to serve as a key logistics center, supported by its distinct port infrastructure and ongoing development. As a premier logistics hub, Baltimore offers essential last-mile delivery access to densely populated markets along the Eastern Seaboard. Major companies like Amazon benefit from the Port of Baltimore’s shipping and rail capabilities.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

Baltimore is considered an attractive market for investors. The region continues to serve as a key logistics center, supported by its distinct port infrastructure and ongoing development. As a premier logistics hub, Baltimore offers essential last-mile delivery access to densely populated markets along the Eastern Seaboard. Major companies like Amazon benefit from the Port of Baltimore’s shipping and rail capabilities.

description:
NAI Columbia is pleased to present 5300 West Main Street in Belleville, Illinois, an opportunity for an owner/occupier or value-add investor.
 
This 2,600-square-foot vacant medical office building comprises a single story, allowing buyers to optimize the floor plate. The property features a simple brick façade, providing a low-maintenance exterior and a timeless aesthetic for businesses. Monument signage is included, offering an ideal opportunity for business branding and customer attraction. Situated on a 0.52-acre lot, ample parking is available for both patients and staff. Constructed in 1979, the building has been consistently well-maintained and is ready for immediate occupancy. Formerly occupied by Affordable Dentistry Today (Heartland Dental as DSO), the property presents significant potential for dental, medical, healthcare, or related uses. The facility is configured with six treatment rooms, reception stations, a break room, and private offices. Buyers have the opportunity to utilize the space for their own business and build out to their specifications. Alternatively, this can be an investment opportunity, and purchasers can lease out at market rates. 
 
The property is situated just off Frank Scott Parkway on West Main Street, directly across from Althoff Catholic High School. Its proximity to Memorial Hospital Belleville, a Magnet-designated facility, enhances its appeal by providing strong medical synergy. The site offers excellent connectivity and is located near several retailers and businesses that contribute to the area's vibrant activity. Nearby establishments include The Abbey, Rally’s, and Papa Vito’s Pizza West. Additionally, major national retailers such as McDonald’s, CVS, and Subway further increase visibility and attract consistent traffic to the location.
 
The area offers favorable demographics for buyers, with a stable population within 5 miles earning an average of $85,122 per year. Most households are owner-occupied, presenting opportunities for buyers. The local workforce totals 44,441, and consumer spending averages over $1.15 billion annually. These factors indicate a vibrant community where buyers can feel confident about investing. 
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

brokerDetails.title: Director of Retail Services | Principal
brokerDetails.bio:
Patrick Palmer is a Principal and Broker with NAI Columbia and has extensive experience across the southeast in brokerage and development. Palmer’s primary focus is retail and general commercial. Before joining NAI Columbia, Palmer served as President and Broker in Charge of IAP Company, a family-owned real estate enterprise that has developed real estate for over 50 years in Columbia. Palmer has been a member of ICSC since 2000, and in 2006, he obtained the prestigious CCIM (Certified Commercial Investment Member) award and subsequently served on several national committees and now has a seat on CCIM’s National Board of Directors.

From 2003 to 2014, Palmer served on the Richland County Planning Commission where he chaired the commission for six of those years. In 2022, Palmer transacted over 37 million dollars in CRE sales and leases and has been named the Retail Leasing Power Broker of the Year and Quarter multiple times, most recently in 2022. Over the past 23 years, Patrick’s real estate experience included: landlord/tenant representation; site selection; land acquisition; disposition and development; buyer/seller representation; investment sales; and zoning and permitting processing.

brokerDetails.title: Broker
brokerDetails.bio: Bobby joined NAI as a brokerage associate in June 2019 after interning with the firm during his junior and senior year at the University of South Carolina. Bobby works alongside Patrick Palmer, Principal and Director of Retail Services. Their team’s focus is on retail and general commercial properties in the Midlands. Bobby specializes in landlord, seller and tenant representation. Bobby graduated Summa Cum Laude from the University of South Carolina in May of 2019, where he studied Finance and Real Estate. During his undergrad years Bobby tutored peers in finance and accounting courses, served as the Sports Editor for The Daily Gamecock, and was a member of the Carolina Finance & Investment Association. Before moving to Columbia, Bobby grew up on Buzzards Bay in southeastern Massachusetts.

description:
As the exclusive advisor, Coldwell Banker Commercial River Valley presents the 54,500-square-foot commercial center at 400 W 9th Street in Ladysmith’s downtown area and Opportunity Zone.
    
The center comprises a big-box space and 7,500 square feet of three occupied in-line retail storefronts. This offering can cater to a retail/distribution/manufacturing user looking to secure expansive commercial space with high ceilings while offsetting expenses with in-place rental income and leases with extension opportunities. An additional in-place stream of income comes from a month-to-month parking lot agreement charging $125/month. Loading capabilities are supported by two standard-height loading docks with seals and vertical hydraulic dock levelers, as well as an exterior cube van dock. The subject spaces neighbor a Tractor Supply Co. building and share multiple points of access.
    
Downtown Ladysmith’s commercial synergy stems from national retailers and regional centers. Walmart Supercenter, Health Mart Pharmacy, Lamperts, and Dollar General are some of the larger tenants along the corridor. US Route 8 passes through the community, offering an east-west highway across Wisconsin and leading about 39 miles west to Rice Lake. Wisconsin Highway 27 makes up the bulk of the 64-mile drive to Eau Claire. The Marshfield Medical Center, just off Highway 27, is Rusk County’s regional hospital health system. 
    
While retailers are present in the downtown core, major employers throughout Rusk County operate in flex/industrial areas. Weather Shield Manufacturing Inc., Rockwell Automation, Artisans Inc., and BiOrigin Products are the backbone of the local industrial sector and provide significant employment. 
    
According to CoStar analytics, residents within a 10-mile radius of the subject have an average household income of nearly $65,000 annually. Education and health services, manufacturing, trade, and transportation and utilities reflect the three largest daytime employee counts. Among consumer spending categories, transportation and maintenance are the largest, followed by food and beverages.

description:
914-916 Tonsor Road provides a versatile industrial facility spanning ±15,000 square feet with a well-connected location in Alton, Illinois. Offered vacant, the property delivers an immediate lease or occupancy upside, with potential for significant long-term appreciation through redevelopment. 
 
Originally built in 1990, the property comprises two metal buildings situated on three contiguous parcels totaling 3.26 acres. Zoned M-1, the facility supports a range of industrial applications, including manufacturing, logistics, warehousing, and more. This large, fenced yard area also offers outdoor storage capacity, with above-ground diesel tanks for fleet and contractor use. The buildings are equipped with 10 oversized drive-in doors and a 16-foot clear height, allowing for seamless loading and unloading.
 
914-916 Tonsor Road benefits from an easily accessible location just off SR 3 and SR 67, with convenient connections to Interstate 255 and 270. Nearby State Route 3 provides a lively retail corridor, enhancing appeal with a wealth of national brands such as Target, Lowe’s, Planet Fitness, Harbor Freight, Kohl’s, Starbucks, Jimmy John’s, Applebee’s, Panera Bread, and more. Tenants will also be able to quickly reach world-class freight support at Norfolk Southern Luther Yard (19.6 miles), the Terminal Railroad Association of St. Louis (21.8 miles), and St. Louis Lambert International Airport (21.3 miles), allowing for streamlined movement of goods and materials across the greater region. 
 
Alton sits within the Illinois Submarket of St. Louis, a rapidly emerging industrial area with more than 1.1 million square feet of new inventory constructed during the past 12 months. The area’s appeal is driven by proximity to Downtown St. Louis and major highways, as well as strong demographics. Looking within 10 miles of 900-916 Tonsor Road, the area offers a population of more than 148,000 and a daytime workforce of more than 46,500. Over 22% of the local workforce is employed in blue-collar fields, providing highly qualified talent to support growth for industrial businesses. 
 
Combining quality infrastructure, an easily accessible location, and long-term growth opportunities, 900-916 Tonsor Road is the perfect acquisition to enter or expand in Madison County. 
 
For those seeking a portfolio of scale in this vibrant market, a nearby property available for auction at 946 Tonsor Road provides an additional 16,000 square feet of functional industrial space. Delivered vacant, this property offers immediate leasing or occupancy potential, along with possibilities for long-term appreciation through redevelopment.
 
Constructed in 2016, the site features two metal buildings positioned on a 1.97-acre lot, providing outdoor storage capacity and extensive on-site surface parking. Zoned M-1, the facility caters to a diverse range of industrial uses, including manufacturing, logistics, and warehousing, and the expansive yard is ideally suited for equipment or fleet staging. With a total of 12 oversized drive-in doors and 16-foot clear heights, the buildings ensure efficient loading and unloading for a wide variety of operational needs.
 
946 Tonsor Road benefits from an easily accessible location just off SR 3 and SR 67, with convenient connections to Interstate 255 and 270. Nearby State Route 3 provides a lively retail corridor, enhancing appeal with a wealth of national brands such as Target, Lowe’s, Planet Fitness, Harbor Freight, Kohl’s, Starbucks, Jimmy John’s, Applebee’s, Panera Bread, and more. Tenants will also be able to quickly reach world-class freight support at Norfolk Southern Luther Yard (19.6 miles), the Terminal Railroad Association of St. Louis (21.8 miles), and St. Louis Lambert International Airport (21.3 miles), allowing for streamlined movement of goods and materials across the greater region. 
 
Alton sits within the Illinois Submarket of St. Louis, a rapidly emerging industrial area with more than 1.1 million square feet of new inventory constructed during the past 12 months. The area’s appeal is driven by proximity to Downtown St. Louis and major highways, as well as strong demographics. Looking within 10 miles of 900-914 Tonsor Road, the area offers a population of more than 148,000 and a daytime workforce of more than 46,500. Over 22% of the local workforce is employed in blue-collar fields, providing highly qualified talent to support growth for industrial businesses.  
 
Providing a unique blend of functional specs and seamless regional connectivity, 946 Tonsor Road offers the perfect entry point into this dynamic market. 
 
For those seeking a portfolio of scale in this vibrant market, a nearby property available for auction at 900-916 Tonsor Road provides an additional 15,000 square feet of functional industrial space. View the links section to view the other opportunity and contact the CBRE team for additional details. 
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
NAI Columbia is pleased to present 50 North Center Street in East Alton, Illinois, an opportunity for an owner/user or value-add investor. 
 
Located off N Center Street, this property offers ample parking and prominent street signage, providing a valuable opportunity for businesses seeking visibility. The building boasts a durable and classic brick façade, designed for longevity. As a single-story structure, the space efficiently utilizes its 1,125 square feet, making it suitable for a variety of business types. Formerly occupied by Heartland Dental (DSO), the premises are particularly well-suited for dental, medical, healthcare, or related uses. Owner/users have the flexibility to redesign and customize the space to fit their requirements, while investors may realize value-add opportunities through capital improvements and leasing at competitive market rates. This asset presents significant potential for both end users and investors.  
 
Situated in East Alton, this property lies approximately 11 miles from the Interstates 270 and 255 interchange, offering seamless connectivity throughout the greater St. Louis metropolitan area. The location is just minutes from St. Louis Regional Airport, which attracts steady traffic to the community. Nearby, residents and visitors have access to a variety of local establishments as well as prominent national retailers, including Walmart Supercenter, Applebee’s Grill + Bar, McDonald’s, Taco Bell, Ace Hardware, and others. Furthermore, the area's demographics are strengthened by a predominantly owner-occupied housing presence and a workforce exceeding 22,000 employees within a 5-mile radius.
 
Being within the Greater St. Louis MSA gives this property considerable advantages. St. Louis features advanced manufacturing, bioscience, and financial services, and serves as an important transportation and logistics center thanks to its location on the Mississippi River. The area is also home to 16 Fortune 1000 companies, strengthening the local economy.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

brokerDetails.name: Patrick Palmer
brokerDetails.tagLine: Retail Leasing & Sales
brokerDetails.title: Director of Retail Services | Principal
brokerDetails.bio:
Patrick Palmer is a Principal and Broker with NAI Columbia and has extensive experience across the southeast in brokerage and development. Palmer’s primary focus is retail and general commercial. Before joining NAI Columbia, Palmer served as President and Broker in Charge of IAP Company, a family-owned real estate enterprise that has developed real estate for over 50 years in Columbia. Palmer has been a member of ICSC since 2000, and in 2006, he obtained the prestigious CCIM (Certified Commercial Investment Member) award and subsequently served on several national committees and now has a seat on CCIM’s National Board of Directors.

From 2003 to 2014, Palmer served on the Richland County Planning Commission where he chaired the commission for six of those years. In 2022, Palmer transacted over 37 million dollars in CRE sales and leases and has been named the Retail Leasing Power Broker of the Year and Quarter multiple times, most recently in 2022. Over the past 23 years, Patrick’s real estate experience included: landlord/tenant representation; site selection; land acquisition; disposition and development; buyer/seller representation; investment sales; and zoning and permitting processing.

brokerDetails.name: Bobby Balboni, CCIM
brokerDetails.title: Broker
brokerDetails.bio: Bobby joined NAI as a brokerage associate in June 2019 after interning with the firm during his junior and senior year at the University of South Carolina. Bobby works alongside Patrick Palmer, Principal and Director of Retail Services. Their team’s focus is on retail and general commercial properties in the Midlands. Bobby specializes in landlord, seller and tenant representation. Bobby graduated Summa Cum Laude from the University of South Carolina in May of 2019, where he studied Finance and Real Estate. During his undergrad years Bobby tutored peers in finance and accounting courses, served as the Sports Editor for The Daily Gamecock, and was a member of the Carolina Finance & Investment Association. Before moving to Columbia, Bobby grew up on Buzzards Bay in southeastern Massachusetts.

brokerDetails.name: Sam Edens
brokerDetails.tagLine: Works closely with the Carolina Shopping Center Strategists team, focusing on retail and general commercial properties in the Midlands.
brokerDetails.title: Brokerage Associate
brokerDetails.bio: Born and raised in Columbia, South Carolina, Sam enjoys outdoor activities such as golfing and hunting. He joined NAI Columbia in July of 2024 upon graduating with a major in Retail Management, at the University of South Carolina. After interning with Patrick Palmer and Bobby Balboni for half a year, he has officially joined as a member of the team, leveraging their diverse range of experience to cater to their clients’ needs across the Midlands.

brokerDetails.name: Michael Williamson, CCIM
brokerDetails.title: Senior Broker Associate
brokerDetails.bio:
Mike is a graduate of the University of Iowa, where he earned his BA degree in Interdepartmental Studies of Business and holds a certification of Entrepreneurship. He joined David Ellermann, CCIM, CIPS and SperryCGA in November of 2017 to start and grow the charter Chicago office.

In 2014, Mike started his career with Premier Commercial Realty and in just under two short years, was involved in over 40 transactions. In April 2016, received his CCIM (Certified Commercial Investment Member) designation by the age of 25. 

Mike now holds a seat on the 2018 CCIM Illinois Board of Directors along with partner, and 2017 CCIM Illinois President, David Ellermann.

description:
NAI Columbia is pleased to present 515 N Monroe Street, an opportunity for an owner/occupier or value-add investor. This property presents a remarkable opportunity for a 1,058-square-foot medical office building on a spacious 0.08-acre lot. Constructed in 1962 and carefully maintained, it boasts a classic brick exterior complemented by an awning perfect for prominent signage. Previously home to Affordable Dentistry Today (Heartland Dental as DSO), the site is ideally suited for dental, medical, healthcare, or similar businesses. Buyers have the flexibility to establish their own practice or lease the space at market rates, maximizing net operating income.
 
Situated close to HSHS St. Francis Hospital, the site benefits from significant medical synergy, with nearly 240 local residents employed at the facility. The hospital is part of the Hospital Sisters Health System (HSHS), comprising 15 hospitals across Illinois and Wisconsin. Litchfield is located approximately 50 miles northeast of St. Louis and 45 miles south of Springfield. Recognized as a historic location along US Route 66, the city features attractions such as the Litchfield Museum & Route 66 Welcome Center. The surrounding area is highly active, featuring an array of retailers and dining establishments that contribute to substantial daily traffic near the property. Within one block, businesses including Domino’s Pizza, Kismet Coffee, and Route 66 BBQ and Southern Kitchen offer additional amenities. Prominent nearby retailers such as Dollar General, USPS, Dairy Queen, Rise & Grind Coffee, Taco Bell, Wendy’s, and others further enhance consumer draw to the area.
 
Buyers benefit from excellent demographics near this property, with the surrounding area experiencing growth within a 10-mile radius. The average household income is $71,389, contributing to a total spending power of $282 million. More than 11,000 people are employed locally, and most households are owner-occupied, reflecting a strong, stable population.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
6760 Foster Bridge Boulevard is a standout opportunity presenting investors and owner/users with a premium manufacturing warehouse designed to exceed expectations.
 
This 27,000-square-foot building is designed for durability and offers a host of impressive features. The single-story layout fully utilizes the available space on its 0.96-acre lot, originally constructed in 1984. Inside, the property features 24-foot clear heights and a robust power distribution system, ideal for seamless, large-scale operations. Security and privacy are ensured by a fully fenced and gated perimeter. Outside, an expansive 18,000-square-foot front parking lot provides ample room for parking, storage, or creative configurations. Additionally, the oversized grade-level door, measuring 18 feet wide by 14 feet, 6 inches high, facilitates efficient loading.
 
This location offers exceptional access to the 710 and 5 Freeways, ensuring efficient logistics and a wide market reach across Los Angeles. For investors, this means streamlined operations and strong growth potential.
 
Nearby retailers and restaurants, including Taco Bell and Dollar Tree, add convenience for employees and boost the property’s appeal. With robust infrastructure and strategic positioning, this facility stands out as a prime investment with promising long-term returns.
 
Bell Gardens, strategically positioned in the heart of Southern California’s dynamic manufacturing and logistics corridor, is rapidly attracting attention from forward-thinking business leaders and investors. The area’s prime accessibility and robust industrial infrastructure create the perfect environment for companies aiming to streamline operations and maximize efficiency. As demand for industrial assets in Bell Gardens continues to outpace the limited supply, property values are set for impressive long-term growth. 
 
This outstanding facility presents a rare opportunity for investors and owner/users alike to secure a top-tier manufacturing warehouse featuring exceptional privacy, security, and operational advantages. 
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
Qualified investors have the exclusive opportunity to acquire a 100% fee simple interest in the Delaware Water Gap Travel Plaza, a Sunoco-anchored service station plaza encompassing a 21,780-square-foot retail building and a 12-pump gas station at 17-19 Simpson Road in Columbia, New Jersey.
    
The retail building is currently 100% master leased by Applegreen who operates a Chick-Fil-A, Strbucks, and Auntie Anne's out of this location. The well-maintained interior features a large food court with a skylit atrium ceiling, providing overhead daylighting and ceiling fans. A large, mounted TV displays weather and news for travelers to observe. 
    
The plaza is located on Interstate 80 near the border of New Jersey and Pennsylvania, right next to the Delaware Water Gap. It is the first privately owned Travel Plaza in the state of New Jersey and is the first rest stop from New York City on the route west to Pennsylvania. This area is booming year-round. The summer attracts travelers who hike and boat in the Delaware Water Gap, as well as those who come to Pennsylvania for its various water parks. Winter attracts skiing and snowboarding in Pennsylvania’s Pocono Mountains. The plaza recently added 8 Tesla Super-Chargers EV Charging Stations.
    
The service station generates a healthy Net Operating Income (NOI) of over $975,000 per year from rental income and sales revenue. Inquire today to learn more about this stable investment.
 
Please reach out to Brian Anderson for financing inquiries.

Qualified investors have the exclusive opportunity to acquire a 100% fee simple interest in the Delaware Water Gap Travel Plaza, a Sunoco-anchored service station plaza encompassing a 21,780-square-foot retail building and a 12-pump gas station at 17-19 Simpson Road in Columbia, New Jersey.
    
The retail building is currently 100% master leased by Applegreen who operates a Chick-Fil-A, Strbucks, and Auntie Anne's out of this location. The well-maintained interior features a large food court with a skylit atrium ceiling, providing overhead daylighting and ceiling fans. A large, mounted TV displays weather and news for travelers to observe. 
    
The plaza is located on Interstate 80 near the border of New Jersey and Pennsylvania, right next to the Delaware Water Gap. It is the first privately owned Travel Plaza in the state of New Jersey and is the first rest stop from New York City on the route west to Pennsylvania. This area is booming year-round. The summer attracts travelers who hike and boat in the Delaware Water Gap, as well as those who come to Pennsylvania for its various water parks. Winter attracts skiing and snowboarding in Pennsylvania’s Pocono Mountains. The plaza recently added 8 Tesla Super-Chargers EV Charging Stations.
    
The service station generates a healthy Net Operating Income (NOI) of over $975,000 per year from rental income and sales revenue. Inquire today to learn more about this stable investment.
 
Please reach out to Brian Anderson for financing inquiries.

description:
Fully renovated, 100% occupied multi-tenant investment opportunity.
  
114 W Main Street and 50 Court Street stand out as a distinguished office asset and parking lot, combining timeless architecture with exceptional street presence. Spanning three stories and offering 36,189 square feet, this property has been carefully renovated to balance historic character with modern upgrades. Its grand front staircase welcomes tenants and guests into a bright lobby featuring soaring ceilings and abundant natural light. Inside, flexible suites of varying sizes accommodate a dynamic mix of businesses, creating a collaborative environment that supports strong occupancy and future leasing potential. The Central Business District (CBD) zoning enhances investment value, encouraging high-density, mixed-use growth with design standards that elevate curb appeal and pedestrian experience. With its prime location, modern amenities, and zoning advantages, this portfolio presents investors with a compelling opportunity in New Britain's thriving business core.
  
Nestled in the heart of New Britain’s vibrant downtown, this location offers investors a unique advantage. Renowned as the Hardware City, the district blends historic 19th-century architecture with an exciting mix of dining, culture, and entertainment. Downtown is undergoing a dynamic revitalization. The proximity to major cultural landmarks such as the New Britain Museum of American Art, Hole in the Wall Theatre, and the iconic Soldiers Monument enriches the neighborhood’s appeal. A robust culinary scene, highlighted by local favorites like Five Churches Brewing and a variety of acclaimed Polish and Latin American restaurants, ensures a lively atmosphere. Additionally, the presence of prominent government offices and civic institutions in the vicinity further elevates the desirability of owning office property here, making it a prime choice for forward-thinking investors.
 
Positioned in the Center of Transformational Growth
The property is strategically located within one of the area’s fastest-growing mixed-use corridors, surrounded by significant new residential and retail development:
 
An 80-unit residential project with a rooftop bar is currently under construction adjacent to the building, with a 2027 delivery
The Brit has recently delivered 107 residential units
Highrailer is coming to market with 114 units and restaurant space
The Strand is scheduled for 2027 delivery, adding 101 residential units, plus retail and a bank
An additional ~220 units have been recently delivered or are under construction, with expected completion by 2027
Total: 600+ new residential units driving immediate and long-term demand for retail, office, and service-oriented uses.
  
Future Development Upside
50 Court Street offers as-of-right development potential, providing a rare opportunity for expansion or additional value creation
 
Capital Improvements – 114 Recent Upgrades
 
Exterior Enhancements
New paving throughout the site
Newly constructed retaining wall
Exterior stone fully repaired and acid cleaned
Historic lighting and entry stairs restored and refurbished
New professional landscaping
Architectural lighting added to illuminate the building façade
 
Interior Upgrades
Fully renovated common areas with new LED lighting, carpet, and wall coverings
New elevator cab installation
Restored main lobby with upgraded lighting design
Renovated bathrooms on all three floors
Multiple tenant buildouts completed
New building signage
Efficiency upgrades throughout the property
Modern access control systems installed
 
This offering combines:
Immediate proximity to major residential growth
Significant recent capital investment
Additional development potential
 
…creating a compelling opportunity for investors seeking stability, upside, and long-term appreciation in a rapidly evolving submarket.
  
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

brokerDetails.name: Michael Guidicelli, CCIM, SIOR
brokerDetails.tagLine: Your Trusted Commercial Real Estate Advisor
brokerDetails.title: Broker & Owner
brokerDetails.bio:
Michael stands at the forefront of the investment real estate market as the esteemed Owner and Power Broker of Regions Commercial, LLC. His firm is renowned for its specialization in the sale of intricate investment properties and providing unparalleled tenant representation services. Michael's expertise is backed by his prestigious designation as a CCIM (Certified Commercial Investment Member), a testament to his deep-rooted knowledge and practical experience in commercial and investment real estate.

Throughout his illustrious career, Michael has played a pivotal role in shaping the landscape of the real estate industry. His dedication and leadership have been instrumental in his decade-long service on the Board of Directors for the Connecticut Chapter of CCIM, including two remarkable years as President (2016 & 2017). Under his stewardship, the chapter witnessed exponential growth, nearly doubling its membership and tripling its net operating income. His exceptional leadership skills were further recognized through his role as Regional Vice President of Region 11, overseeing pivotal chapters across the Northeastern United States.

Michael's commitment to excellence is also evident in his attainment of the SIOR designation from The SOCIETY OF INDUSTRIAL AND OFFICE REALTORS®, marking him as a leading authority in commercial real estate brokerage. His tenure as President of the CT/W. MA SIOR Chapter and his continued role as Immediate Past President highlight his significant contributions and steadfast dedication to the industry.

His outstanding achievements have not gone unnoticed, earning him a spot in the top 40 Under 40 by the Hartford Business Journal, and highlighting his remarkable impact in the field. Furthermore, Michael's academic credentials are equally impressive, with a graduate degree from Harvard Law School's Program on Negotiation, equipping him with unrivaled negotiation skills that benefit his clients immensely.

As a staunch advocate for his clients, Michael offers expert guidance through every step of the real estate transaction process. His commitment to his clients is unparalleled, offering both commission-based and consulting services tailored to meet their unique needs. The opportunity to begin with an initial consultation without obligation is a testament to his client-centered approach, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of their requirements from the outset.

Michael's distinguished career, marked by his leadership roles, prestigious designations, and unwavering dedication to his clients, sets him apart as a leading figure in the investment real estate industry.

description:
Discover an unrivaled acquisition in Northwest Dallas at 8700 N Stemmons Freeway, offering a performing multi-tenant office complex with exceptional long-term redevelopment potential. 
 
Currently 70% occupied and anchored by a long-standing (20+ years) GSA lease, this 250,963-square-foot facility presents robust rental income across a diverse tenant mix of law firms, marketing agencies, the Texas Lottery, and public and professional service organizations. Key amenities at the property include abundant covered parking, an on-site restaurant and café, and a towering atrium with a massive skylight and beautiful landscaping, and water features. The building currently boasts a two-year weighted average lease term (WALT) and robust net operating income (NOI), allowing a new owner to capitalize on existing revenue while pursuing long-term opportunities as a covered land play. 
 
The property is situated on a ±9.04-acre site across two contiguous parcels that is currently zoned for multifamily, with added potential for industrial applications. The lot is positioned directly off Interstate 35E, with visibility to over 130,000 daily vehicles, and backs up to Brook Hollow Country Club, making it premium land for future projects in the area. With convenient proximity to the medical and design districts, the surrounding region has emerged as a desirable destination for new developments, driven by stable population and economic growth in recent years. More than $8 billion has been invested in the area’s infrastructure over the past decade, alongside large recent developments across multifamily, industrial, data center, and office segments. 
 
Visit the Data Room and reach out to the Younger Partners Dallas team to learn more about this one-of-a-kind opportunity that combines stable rental revenue and long-term redevelopment potential in a sought-after Dallas submarket at 8700 N Stemmons Freeway. 
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
Open House: Tuesday, April 7th from 12:00 PM - 2:00 PM EST. Please contact the brokers to schedule.
   
3190 Tremont Avenue is a multi-level light industrial building built in 1975 and renovated in 1998 and 2017, ringing in at 72,640 square feet on 6.18 acres. The space breakdown offers 12,560 square feet of office on two levels and approximately 60,000 square feet of light industrial. The light industrial space is made up of approximately 20,000 square feet on the lower level, providing drive and door access, and 40,000 square feet on the ground floor with dock access and the potential for a drive-in if desired. The ceiling heights on the lower level reach 11 feet, and the ground floor reaches 12.5 feet. The property has major curb appeal and a two-story light-filled lobby. Zoned light industrial (L-1), the property presents potential for a wide array of uses. It also comes equipped with several attractive office and flex features already built in, including multiple private restrooms, a kitchenette, and two tailgate-height overhead loading docks.
   
3190 Tremont Avenue presents investors and owner/users with an enticing offer to take over a vacant office building just outside of Philadelphia. The property was occupied by the previous owner and vacated in 2020. It has a current build-out for office use. However, the L-1 zoning allows a wide array of uses, including light manufacturing, scientific research facilities, wholesale business facilities, warehousing and storage, monument production facilities, and more.
   
Just 40 minutes to Philadelphia and teetering on the New Jersey border, this property is in a prime location. In recent years, several firms have relocated from Central New Jersey to the Lower Bucks County submarket. Pre-pandemic healthcare providers and healthcare technology firms, including Capital Health, StayWell, and ETHOS Health Communications, were improving market conditions in the area. The Lower Bucks County submarket has seen an uptick in out-of-market investors acquiring medical, office, and flex properties. Leasing in the submarket has been on the rise over recent years, as healthcare providers and healthcare technology firms, including Capital Health, StayWell, and ETHOS Health Communications, have moved to the area.

The breadth of his market familiarity and depth of knowledge prove exceptionally beneficial to clients and often serve to accelerate cycle time and/or present off-market opportunities. An accomplished leader in NJ/PA commercial real estate, Jeff stepped out onto the global stage and became a leader within NAI Global, a top 5 brand with more than 400 offices and 7,000 professionals. Jeff is well-known, respected and liked throughout the global organization which enables him to place requirements on behalf of clients while providing the confidence that they will be served by a colleague with whom Jeff has a relationship. Jeff’s question to you is, “Where can I help you?” Jeff’s track record of success is more than impressive and includes: 41MM+ Square Feet Successfully Represented for Sale/Lease; 2,000+ Sales and Leases Negotiated and Closed; $1B+ Aggregate Transaction Volume.

description:
As the exclusive agent, Cushman & Wakefield is pleased to offer a 100% fee simple interest in 651–657 US Highway 22 E for sale in Northern New Jersey. The asset comprises a 12,000-square-foot strip center, conveniently located on a 1.15-acre parcel with direct frontage along Route 22 East and multiple access points. 
    
The property is 100% occupied by six commercial tenants: the Salt & Pepper Grill, Tailored Consignment, Sporting Life, Toscanella Restaurant, Broadway Salons, Airs Above Yoga, and the convenience store. There is also one residential tenant in the three-bedroom apartment, which features stainless steel appliances and modern finishes. 651-657 US Highway 22 E was initially built in 1960 and underwent a full gut renovation in 2018, including a new roof, HVAC, electrical, and significant façade work. This offering provides an investor with the opportunity to acquire a newly renovated strip center with value-add upside through lease restructuring and re-tenanting in a growing retail corridor along Route 22.
    
Readington Township (White House Station) boasts a vibrant community nestled within picturesque landscapes. Its accessibility is enhanced by major highways, such as Interstate 78, which facilitate smooth connectivity to neighboring towns and cities. Demographically diverse, the township welcomes a mix of families, professionals, and retirees, contributing to a rich cultural tapestry. 
    
Residents enjoy an array of amenities, including sprawling parks and recreational spaces, top-rated schools, and a bustling downtown. The region's livability is accentuated by a blend of rural charm and modern conveniences, offering a serene environment without compromising on access to essential services. With its excellent schools, robust transportation networks, and abundant leisure activities, Readington Township is an inviting place to call home for individuals seeking a balanced lifestyle in a welcoming community.
    
The Northern New Jersey Market demonstrates strong fundamentals, proven by the Hunterdon County retail vacancy rate below 4%. The market’s average household income exceeds $201,900, generating a robust consumer spending power of $3.5 billion. Northern New Jersey continues to see steady population growth, along with a limited supply, and anticipates rent growth in early 2026.

Please reach out to Brian Anderson for financing inquiries.

brokerDetails.name: Rick Levin
brokerDetails.tagLine: Mr. Levin is President of Rick Levin & Associates, Inc (RLA). He has been in the auction business since 1990.
brokerDetails.title: Auctioneer / Broker
brokerDetails.bio:
Mr. Levin is President of Rick Levin & Associates, Inc (RLA). He has been in the auction business since 1990. Mr. Levin is a licensed real estate broker in Illinois and a general member of the National Association of Realtors®, Illinois Association of Realtors®, National Auctioneer’s Association, and was also a member of the National Association of Realtors® Auction Committee.

Mr. Levin has experience in all phases of the auction business, ranging from a variety of real estate and personal property auctions, including vacant land; commercial office buildings; luxury single family homes; developer close-outs of homesites, townhomes, condominiums and single family homes, vacation properties; processing plants; and shopping centers to personal property auctions for antiques, aircraft, boats, memorabilia, numerous varieties of vehicles and heavy construction equipment. These sales have been conducted on behalf of a variety of clients, including the Internal Revenue Service, the Chicago Housing Authority, US Bankruptcy Trustees, Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, the US Army, Bank of America, Mcdonalds Restaurants, Harris Bank, Chase Bank, University of Chicago, state governmental departments, municipal corporations, local school boards, quasi-governmental organizations, non-profits, banks, institutional lenders, insurance companies, retailers, investment corporations, developers and individuals.

In 2000, Mr. Levin was appointed by the Governor of Illinois to sit on the Illinois Auction Advisory Board, a division of the Office of Banks and Real Estates, which oversees the administration of the Illinois Auction License Act. The License Act regulates the auctioneers who are licensed with the State of Illinois. Mr. Levin was also appointed in 2005 to the Advisory Board of the School of Real Estate at Roosevelt University in Chicago.

Mr. Levin graduated in 1986 from the School of Business of the University of Illinois-Champaign/Urbana with a business degree in marketing.

description:
Eclipse Apartments at 2641 W Butler Drive is a 75-unit, garden-style apartment community established in 1972 and ideally located in Phoenix, Arizona. The attractive unit mix consists of 60% two-bedroom, 20% one-bedroom, and 20% studio units, averaging 800 square feet. Each apartment features stainless steel appliances, refurbished kitchens, and vinyl plank flooring. Eclipse Apartments contain four garden-style apartment buildings situated on a 2.62-acre site. The community includes a pool, laundry facility, office, playground, and desert landscaping throughout. 
                
Situated on Butler Drive, residents enjoy direct access to all areas of Phoenix via Interstate 17. The Eclipse Apartments are also close to the Light Rail, providing car-free commutes throughout the Valley of the Sun. Residents will find an abundance of conveniences and attractions at Desert Sky Mall, El Oso Park, and several other shopping centers, each filled with restaurants and retailers. 
               
Nestled in the heart of the Phoenix metro, this property is in a safe, welcoming suburban community just 12 miles west of downtown. The neighborhood offers a comfortable atmosphere of residential charm, providing an ideal setting for families, professionals, and students. Nearby schools, such as Maryvale High School, provide quality K-12 education, while Grand Canyon University offers higher education opportunities and a vibrant campus life. With well-maintained parks, shopping centers, and public transit options close at hand, this location offers the best of the suburbs with easy access to the city.
